name: gdpr
description: GDPR-specific rules for code, security, and PII handling. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition (base + regimes + domain + project) per ADR-079.

patterns.description: IBAN — international bank account number (BBAN length varies by country, 11-30 alphanumerics after the 4-char prefix).

severity_rules.pattern: Logger or print statement emits PII fields (email, phone, full name, national id, IP address, IBAN) in plaintext.
severity_rules.description: Logging PII in plaintext breaches GDPR data-minimisation and storage-limitation principles and creates an exfiltration vector via log shipping.
severity_rules.remediation: Mask PII at the logger boundary (hash, truncate, or tokenize) or remove the field from the log payload entirely.

severity_rules.pattern: URL path or query string contains PII (e.g., /users/<email>, ?ssn=...) — values land in access logs and HTTP referrers.
severity_rules.description: PII in URLs leaks into proxy logs, browser history, and referrer headers, defeating storage-limitation guarantees.
severity_rules.remediation: Move identifiers into the request body or use opaque tokens; never put PII in path or query parameters.

severity_rules.pattern: Error response body or stack trace echoes PII back to the caller (e.g., `User <email> not found`).
severity_rules.description: PII in error messages exposes data subjects to enumeration attacks and breaches the integrity-and-confidentiality principle.
severity_rules.remediation: Use opaque correlation ids in error messages; log the full context server-side only.

severity_rules.pattern: PII storage call (database insert, cache set, queue publish) without a documented retention or TTL declaration nearby.
severity_rules.description: Indefinite PII retention violates the storage-limitation principle. Every PII store must declare a retention window aligned with its lawful basis.
severity_rules.remediation: Annotate the storage call with a retention policy reference or set an explicit TTL/scheduled deletion job.

severity_rules.pattern: Tracking, analytics, or marketing call fires before a recorded consent check on the request session or user profile.
severity_rules.description: Processing personal data without a recorded lawful basis (consent or legitimate interest) breaches GDPR Art. 6.
severity_rules.remediation: Gate the call on a consent-state check; default to deny when consent is absent.

severity_rules.pattern: Project handling EU PII has no Data Subject Access Request handler or `/data-export` style endpoint.
severity_rules.description: GDPR Art. 15 requires that data subjects can obtain a copy of their personal data on request.
severity_rules.remediation: Implement a DSAR handler that aggregates PII across all systems for a given subject id.

severity_rules.pattern: Outbound HTTP/queue/database call sends PII to a non-EU endpoint without an SCC, adequacy decision, or BCR reference.
severity_rules.description: Transfers of personal data to third countries require Standard Contractual Clauses or an adequacy decision under Art. 44-49.
severity_rules.remediation: Document the transfer mechanism (SCC/BCR/adequacy) at the call site or pseudonymise the payload before transfer.

severity_rules.pattern: Project with PII storage has no delete-by-subject API or background job that propagates erasure to caches, replicas, and search indexes.
severity_rules.description: Art. 17 right-to-erasure requires that deletion propagates to every PII store, including derived caches and analytics warehouses.
severity_rules.remediation: Implement a fan-out deletion job or event that erases the subject across all systems and emits a confirmation log.

severity_rules.pattern: PII passed to analytics, ML training, or third-party processor without pseudonymisation/tokenisation.
severity_rules.description: Art. 25 (data protection by design) recommends pseudonymisation for any non-essential PII processing path.
severity_rules.remediation: Pseudonymise the field (HMAC, deterministic token) before forwarding or restrict the data set to non-identifying attributes.
